name: Neighborhood National Bank
description_short: Nationally-chartered community bank serving San Diego County since 1997, specializing in financial services for small businesses, nonprofits, and underserved communities through CDFI certification.
description_long:
Neighborhood National Bank (NNB) was founded in 1997 and has operated as the oldest community bank in San Diego County. It is a nationally-chartered, federally insured institution with FDIC backing. As of February 2026, the bank reported $226 million in total assets with 30% growth, $3.8 million in net income, and $1.3 million in operating profit for 2025. The bank expanded operations to include a loan production office in Orange County and plans to open a new full-service branch in National City.

Neighborhood National Bank offers business loans (including SBA loans), deposit accounts, online banking through their GoBanking platform, merchant card services, and financial calculators. They serve individuals, small-to-medium sized businesses, and nonprofits across San Diego County. The bank provides affordable, flexible, and accessible financial products tailored to their customer base.

What distinguishes NNB is its certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) status—the only CDFI bank in San Diego County—which reflects its explicit mission to serve low- and moderate-income communities. The bank focuses on providing banking services in neighborhoods often neglected by national and regional banks, particularly South and East San Diego County. Leadership emphasizes community engagement, support for local nonprofits and churches, and financial inclusion in underserved areas.

NNB appears to be a legitimate, well-capitalized community bank with strong recent performance metrics and genuine commitment to its CDFI mission. However, the website provides limited detail on specific loan terms, rates, fees, or lending criteria, which may require direct contact for precise product information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured and backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Only certified CDFI bank in San Diego County, with 25+ years of community service since 1997
Record profitability in 2025 with $3.8M net income and 30% asset growth demonstrating financial stability
SBA loan programs explicitly featured on homepage for small business support
Modern online banking platform (GoBanking) with digital accessibility features and screen-reader support
Merchant card services and financial calculators available for business customers
Expanding physical presence with new branches and loan production offices
cons: Website lacks specific information on loan rates, APRs, fees, or lending terms
No clear online application process visible; customers must contact bank directly for loan qualification details
Limited geographic service area (primarily San Diego County); expansion to Orange County is recent and may have limited capacity
Website does not specify minimum loan amounts, maximum terms, or qualification requirements for business loans
No mention of alternative lending products, lines of credit details, or equipment financing specifics
best_for: Small-to-medium sized businesses in low and moderate-income communities in San Diego and Orange County
Nonprofits and community organizations seeking mission-aligned banking relationships
Businesses historically underserved by national and regional banks
Communities in South and East San Diego County seeking local banking support
diagnosis: Neighborhood National Bank is best for small businesses and nonprofits in San Diego County seeking community-focused financing with genuine commitment to underserved populations. The primary caveat is that detailed lending terms, rates, and qualification criteria are not available on the website, requiring direct contact with the bank for specific product information and application.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
